$comment: D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12 (FC12 deferral-2) runtime witness: a function whose frame (36000B, `int big[9000]`) exceeds the AArch64 single-word imm12 reach (4095). The prologue/epilogue SP adjust AND the &big[8999] address (sp+35996) must use the SCRATCH-FREE 2-word shifted-imm12 macro (`op Xd,Xn,#lo` + `op Xd,Xd,#hi,LSL #12`). seed is a mutable global so the store→load round-trip survives ConstFold/Mem2Reg/DCE. Exit 42. The `{shippedPipeline:release}` arm is what surfaces the lea-35996 + sp-adjust split under the REAL release composition (Inlining ON); the full-release-like arm exercises the same under an explicit pass list. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the encoder's imm12.hilo24 split (or the variant immMin/immMax routing) → the >4095 sp-adjust + lea fail loud at the assembler (A_ImmediateOperandOutOfRange) and the arm64 leg cannot produce a binary. NOTE on the missing x86_64:pe (Windows) target: a >4KB Windows stack frame needs an inline guard-page stack-probe loop in the prologue (D-WIN64-LARGE-FRAME-STACK-PROBE) — now CLOSED, covered by its OWN example `large_frame_win64` (the `stack_probe` LIR op, x86_64-only; NOT __chkstk — dss PE exes are freestanding). This arm64-only example deliberately does NOT add a pe64 leg (the Windows large-frame witness lives in large_frame_win64); x86_64 has no imm12 slot so its codegen here is byte-identical to before. x86_64:elf (Linux) is the non-Windows control leg (Linux auto-grows the stack via demand paging, absorbing the 9-page jump with no probe).
